title: The Great Society
description: Practice your skills in this quiz on the Great Society.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“In the 1950’s this America worried about itself, yet even its anxieties were products of abundance. . . Now the American city has been transformed. The poor still inhabit the miserable housing in the central areas, but are increasingly isolated from contact with, or sight of, anybody else. . . . [T]he poor are politically invisible. It is one of the cruelest ironies of social life in advanced countries that the dispossessed at the bottom of society are unable to speak for themselves. The people of the other America do not, by far and large, belong to unions, to fraternal organizations, or to political parties. They are without lobbies of their own; they put forward no legislative program. As a group, they are atomized. They have no face; they have no voice . . .”

>-Source: Michael Harrington, *The Other America: Poverty in the United States,* 1962

**The author’s account in the excerpt above most directly encouraged which of the following changes in subsequent years?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the creation of government programs to combat poverty and social issues
the introduction of legislation mandating a federal minimum wage
the elimination of poll taxes and literacy tests in federal elections
the rise of political machines to address the needs of impoverished neighborhoods

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“In the 1950’s this America worried about itself, yet even its anxieties were products of abundance. . . Now the American city has been transformed. The poor still inhabit the miserable housing in the central areas, but are increasingly isolated from contact with, or sight of, anybody else. . . . [T]he poor are politically invisible. It is one of the cruelest ironies of social life in advanced countries that the dispossessed at the bottom of society are unable to speak for themselves. The people of the other America do not, by far and large, belong to unions, to fraternal organizations, or to political parties. They are without lobbies of their own; they put forward no legislative program. As a group, they are atomized. They have no face; they have no voice . . .”

>-Source: Michael Harrington, *The Other America: Poverty in the United States,* 1962

**A historian would most likely use this passage to illustrate which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rising concerns about widespread poverty and efforts to raise it as a national issue
the economic prosperity that led to a mass culture based on consumerism and conformity
the social and economic effects of World War II on the American manufacturing industry
the influence of political fundraising on legislation and goals for presidential administrations

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“In the 1950’s this America worried about itself, yet even its anxieties were products of abundance. . . Now the American city has been transformed. The poor still inhabit the miserable housing in the central areas, but are increasingly isolated from contact with, or sight of, anybody else. . . . [T]he poor are politically invisible. It is one of the cruelest ironies of social life in advanced countries that the dispossessed at the bottom of society are unable to speak for themselves. The people of the other America do not, by far and large, belong to unions, to fraternal organizations, or to political parties. They are without lobbies of their own; they put forward no legislative program. As a group, they are atomized. They have no face; they have no voice . . .”

>-Source: Michael Harrington, *The Other America: Poverty in the United States,* 1962

**The sentiments expressed in the excerpt prompted Congress to take which of the following actions?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: expand the government’s role in order to address the causes of poverty
reduce government spending to reallocate funds to impoverished groups
decrease taxes on all tax brackets to stimulate job creation and economic growth
eliminate costly government programs such as the Social Security Administration

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

> “The task of public authority, like that of private individuals, will be to distribute this increase in accordance with relative need. Schools and road will then no longer be at a disadvantage . . . The practical solution would be much eased were the revenues of the federal government available for the service of social balance. . . . The test is not that high military costs make reductions in other public outlays necessary. Rather, it is whether, given these military outlays (which may be regretted), we are more in need of the services that improve social balance or the additional private goods with which we are more affluently supplied than ever before.”

>-Source: John Kenneth Galbraith, economist, *The Affluent Society,* 1958

**The excerpt most directly reflects a growing belief after 1945 that:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the government was the best entity to address social and economic issues.
the government should practice a laissez-faire economic policy.
the government should reduce the size of the military-industrial complex.
the government could win the arms race by improving education.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

> “The task of public authority, like that of private individuals, will be to distribute this increase in accordance with relative need. Schools and road will then no longer be at a disadvantage . . . The practical solution would be much eased were the revenues of the federal government available for the service of social balance. . . . The test is not that high military costs make reductions in other public outlays necessary. Rather, it is whether, given these military outlays (which may be regretted), we are more in need of the services that improve social balance or the additional private goods with which we are more affluently supplied than ever before.”

>-Source: John Kenneth Galbraith, economist, *The Affluent Society,* 1958

**Ideas similar to those expressed in the passage directly contributed to the development of which of the following aspects in the 1960s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the creation of domestic programs that combat poverty and promote social welfare
the establishment of the Social Security Administration to support the elderly population
the rise of civil rights movements aimed at promoting social and economic equality
the reduction in military spending and taxes on businesses to support economic growth

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

> “The task of public authority, like that of private individuals, will be to distribute this increase in accordance with relative need. Schools and road will then no longer be at a disadvantage . . . The practical solution would be much eased were the revenues of the federal government available for the service of social balance. . . . The test is not that high military costs make reductions in other public outlays necessary. Rather, it is whether, given these military outlays (which may be regretted), we are more in need of the services that improve social balance or the additional private goods with which we are more affluently supplied than ever before.”

>-Source: John Kenneth Galbraith, economist, *The Affluent Society,* 1958

**Which of the following movements expressed ideas most similar to the ideas expressed in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Progressivism in the early 1900s
Conservation in the 1900s
Temperance in the 1820s
Nativism in the 1840s and 1850s

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“The challenge of the next half century is whether we have the wisdom to use [our] wealth to enrich and elevate our national life and to advance the quality of our American civilization. 

>“Your imagination, your initiative, and your indignation will determine whether we build a society where progress is the servant of our needs or a society where old values and new visions are buried under unbridled growth. For in your time we have the opportunity to move not only toward the rich society and the powerful society, but upward to the Great Society.”

>-President Lyndon B. Johnson, Commencement speech at the University of Michigan, 1964

**The policies described in the excerpt contributed most directly to debates in the United States about the:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: role of government in solving social and economic issues.
reduced power of the presidency to check the other branches.
lack of voting power for 18- to 21-year-olds in elections.
American intervention in the affairs of other countries.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“The challenge of the next half century is whether we have the wisdom to use [our] wealth to enrich and elevate our national life and to advance the quality of our American civilization. 

>“Your imagination, your initiative, and your indignation will determine whether we build a society where progress is the servant of our needs or a society where old values and new visions are buried under unbridled growth. For in your time we have the opportunity to move not only toward the rich society and the powerful society, but upward to the Great Society.”

>-President Lyndon B. Johnson, Commencement speech at the University of Michigan, 1964

**Which of the following was a key difference between the New Deal and the Great Society?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Great Society had policies that addressed racial inequalities, while the New Deal did not.
The Great Society had policies that addressed environmental issues, while the New Deal sold government land to businesses.
The New Deal had job training programs for impoverished people, while the Great Society did not.
The New Deal established healthcare programs for young and elderly people, while the Great Society struck them down.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below. 

>“The challenge of the next half century is whether we have the wisdom to use [our] wealth to enrich and elevate our national life and to advance the quality of our American civilization. 

>“Your imagination, your initiative, and your indignation will determine whether we build a society where progress is the servant of our needs or a society where old values and new visions are buried under unbridled growth. For in your time we have the opportunity to move not only toward the rich society and the powerful society, but upward to the Great Society.”

>-President Lyndon B. Johnson, Commencement speech at the University of Michigan, 1964

**The Great Society, as described in the excerpt, has the most in common with which of the following earlier domestic policy initiatives?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The New Deal
The Return to Normalcy
The American System
The Square Deal

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the chart and answer the question below.

![Chart showing immigrant share of total US population from 1900 to 2000. The percentage of foreign-born people in the United States is at its highest in 1910, making up 14.7 percent of the population. It continues to decrease until the percentage of foreign-born people in the United States is 4.7 percent of the population in 1970. After 1970, the percentage of foreign-born population increases every decade, reaching 11.1 percent in 2000.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/322e52f8a7803613489cdff7784e3609.svg)

>Source: U.S. Census Bureau, "[Historical Census Statistics on the Foreign-Born Population of the United States: 1850-2000](https://www.census.gov/prod/2002pubs/p23-206.pdf)," 2002

**A development related to the overall trend from 1920 to 1970 depicted on the graph was the:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: implementation of a national origins quota on incoming immigrants.
failing economy after a major stock market crash and deflation of the US dollar.
widespread destruction of infrastructure following World War II.
investigation and deportation of people associated with the Communist Party.

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the chart and answer the question below.

![Chart showing immigrant share of total US population from 1900 to 2000. The percentage of foreign-born people in the United States is at its highest in 1910, making up 14.7 percent of the population. It continues to decrease until the percentage of foreign-born people in the United States is 4.7 percent of the population in 1970. After 1970, the percentage of foreign-born population increases every decade, reaching 11.1 percent in 2000.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/322e52f8a7803613489cdff7784e3609.svg)

>Source: U.S. Census Bureau, "[Historical Census Statistics on the Foreign-Born Population of the United States: 1850-2000](https://www.census.gov/prod/2002pubs/p23-206.pdf)," 2002

**Which of the following was a significant cause of the trend from 1970 to 2000 shown in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the passage of the Immigration and Nationality Act
the establishment of the *Bracero* program
the ratification of the Twenty-fourth Amendment
the rise of proxy wars in Western Europe

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the chart and answer the question below.

![Chart showing immigrant share of total US population from 1900 to 2000. The percentage of foreign-born people in the United States is at its highest in 1910, making up 14.7 percent of the population. It continues to decrease until the percentage of foreign-born people in the United States is 4.7 percent of the population in 1970. After 1970, the percentage of foreign-born population increases every decade, reaching 11.1 percent in 2000.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/322e52f8a7803613489cdff7784e3609.svg)


>Source: U.S. Census Bureau, "[Historical Census Statistics on the Foreign-Born Population of the United States: 1850-2000](https://www.census.gov/prod/2002pubs/p23-206.pdf)," 2002.

**Which of the following best explains the overall demographic trend shown in the chart?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in the percentage of the population who are immigrants since 1960
A decline in Latin American immigrants between 1920 and 1960.
An increase in the total number of immigrants since 1960
A decrease in the total immigrant population between 1920 and 1960
